name: github-pr-issue
description: 查看/更新 GitHub Issue、PR（含评论与 diff），并按团队规范非交互创建或修改 PR；涉及 GitHub Issue/PR 的操作时使用。
capabilities: View GitHub PR details with optional diff, comments, reviews, and stats
Create GitHub Issues non-interactively using team standards
Create GitHub PRs with semantic title and standardized description
Update Issue and PR titles or descriptions
Export PR information as structured YAML output
Check GitHub API rate limit status
author: DCjanus
securitySummary: Low-risk skill that uses legitimate GitHub CLI commands and API calls to github.com. The external_commands pattern at read_pr.py:54 calls 'gh auth token' with hardcoded arguments for authentication. Network calls only target api.github.com. All cryptographic algorithm and obfuscation findings are false positives - the scanner misidentified Pydantic model field names (totalCount) as crypto functions. The backtick references in SKILL.md are documentation syntax, not executable code. This is a standard developer tool for GitHub workflow automation.
valueStatement: Viewing GitHub Issues and PRs requires multiple CLI commands. This skill provides unified commands to view, create, and update Issues and PRs following team standards. Streamline your GitHub workflow with standardized PR descriptions and non-interactive Issue creation.
userTitle: View and manage GitHub Issues and PRs
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI (gh) to be installed and authenticated
Only supports github.com domain PRs and Issues
Does not create or modify PR review comments
Rate limits apply based on GitHub API tier

useCases.title: Quick PR code review
useCases.description: Pull PR details with diff in one command to review changes without opening browser

useCases.title: Standardized PR descriptions
useCases.description: Generate consistent PR descriptions following team conventions with semantic prefixes

useCases.title: Issue tracking workflow
useCases.description: Create and update Issues programmatically in CI/CD pipelines

promptTemplates.title: View PR details
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need to see a pull request
promptTemplates.prompt: View this GitHub PR: https://github.com/OWNER/REPO/pull/123 with diff, stats, and all comments

promptTemplates.title: Create Issue
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a GitHub Issue titled 'feat: add user authentication' with body describing the feature requirement

promptTemplates.title: Create PR
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a PR from branch 'feature/new-login' to main with semantic title 'feat(auth): implement OAuth2 login' and description including summary, key changes, and testing instructions

promptTemplates.title: Update PR title
promptTemplates.prompt: Update PR title to 'fix: resolve memory leak in connection pool' after reviewing the current title

bestPractices: Always use --body-file for multi-line descriptions to avoid shell escaping issues
Include the PR URL as a separate line after successful creation
Review current content before updating any Issue or PR
antiPatterns: Using interactive editors for Issue or PR creation in automated workflows
Skipping the review step before updating titles or descriptions
Making multiple gh API calls when one script execution can gather all needed data

faq.question: What tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, OpenAI Codex, and Claude Code CLI environments.

faq.question: What are the rate limits?
faq.answer: GitHub API limits vary by plan. Free tier: 60 requests per hour. Authenticated: 5,000 per hour.

faq.question: How does authentication work?
faq.answer: The skill uses 'gh auth token' to retrieve your GitHub CLI authentication token automatically.

faq.question: Is my GitHub token stored or logged?
faq.answer: No. Tokens are used only for API requests and never written to logs or disk.

faq.question: Why does the script fail with 'gh command not found'?
faq.answer: GitHub CLI must be installed separately. Install via 'brew install gh' or from github.com/cli

faq.question: How does this differ from GitHub Copilot?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on PR and Issue operations via CLI, while Copilot provides code suggestions.
